mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6.0 percent, consumer spending power contracts sharply and recession fears dominate market pricing, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of periods where UNRATE crosses above 6.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts signal synchronized consumer and business confidence, which amplifies equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, with a hit rate below 45 percent over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to precede positive 5-day returns during early-cycle expansions, with a t-statistic below 1.5 on the predictive coefficient.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth reflects robust aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, which systematically lifts S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows a negative or zero correlation with 60-day forward returns across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and forces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL monthly change crosses above 0.4 percent
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in over 60 percent of months where CPIAUCSL inflation exceeds the 0.4 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal yields a positive return hit rate below 48 percent over a 4-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a manufacturing contraction that reduces corporate profit margins and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth crosses below zero for two consecutive months
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances following two consecutive months of INDPRO declines.
